Moringa oleifera, commonly known as Moringa, is a tropical plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. The leaves, seeds, and pods of Moringa have been reported to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties, as well as being rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids. The bioactive constituents of Moringa include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Moringa is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, as well as being added to food and beverages due to its nutritional value.
